title: Europe once again signals openness to dialogue with Putin
subhead: European countries seek to reopen a channel of communication with Russia, with France first among them, at a time when the US appears to be losing interest in the war in Ukraine.

title: Beyond AI billions, energy emerges as the crucial battleground at Choose France summit
California's primary elections serves as test for divided Democrats
2026 World Cup poses massive security challenge for US law enforcement
subhead: For the first time, the tournament will feature 48 teams and be played across three countries, all amid heightened geopolitical tensions. US authorities are already working behind the scenes to ensure the event runs safely and smoothly.

title: Trump to attend new White House press gala after shooting
subhead: US President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday that he will speak at the White House correspondents dinner, which has been rescheduled for July 24. At this year's edition that was held in April, Trump had to be evacuated after shots erupted at a security checkpoint outside the ballroom venue.

title: 'It's the Palme d'Or of French hospitality': The government unveils its updated list of the 33 most luxurious hotels in France
subhead: The new round of the government's 'Palaces de France' label was revealed on June 2, with six new additions and four removals. The initiative aims to showcase this flagship sector of French expertise, even though the owners of these establishments are rarely French.

title: Violence amid PSG celebrations is punished with prison: 'Are we living in a society of chaos? No'
subhead: On Monday, trials were held for some of the 890 people arrested across France after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League.
byline: Grégoire Biseau,  Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Richard Schittly

Putin faces a series of setbacks, fueling discontent in Russia and raising doubts about his strategy
subhead: While the Russian president claims that victory in Ukraine is within reach, Kyiv is stepping up drone strikes deep inside Russian territory. These military setbacks compound the country's mounting economic difficulties.

title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

title: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
subhead: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.
